parsed.summary: The v0.4.0→v0.4.6 cumulative diff is massive (6,600+ insertions across 84 files) and includes multiple rounds of post-review hotfixes (Bundles C through F+), a deleted module (process.mjs), new complex subsystems (retry.mjs at 203 lines, bigmodel-errors.mjs at 168 lines), and heavy modifications to core client/state/render paths. The multiple hotfix rounds and a ReferenceError purge in Bundle F suggest residual instability. I could not inspect the actual file contents to confirm specific code-level defects, but the structural signals are concerning enough to warrant a detailed line-level review before shipping.

findings.title: Deleted process.mjs (67 lines) risks dangling imports across core modules
findings.body: The diff stat shows scripts/lib/process.mjs was entirely removed (67 lines deleted). Concurrent modifications to glm-companion.mjs, render.mjs, state.mjs, and job-control.mjs suggest imports were rewritten, but any transitive or dynamic import left referencing process.mjs will cause a runtime crash (ModuleNotFoundError). Bundle F's commit message ('ReferenceError + full thread/resume scaffolding purge') suggests prior import-related breakage was already encountered and patched — but the purge itself may have missed edge-case callers (e.g., test files, hooks, or CI scripts that import transitively).
findings.recommendation: Grep the entire codebase (including test files, hooks, CI scripts) for any remaining reference to process.mjs or its exported symbols. Run the full test suite to confirm no missing-module errors. Add an import-lint CI check to prevent future dangling imports.

findings.title: retry.mjs (203 new lines) is a new critical-path module with no proven edge-case handling
findings.body: retry.mjs is entirely new and 203 lines — large enough to contain subtle bugs around backoff calculation, abort handling, and idempotency. The commit history shows it was added in the same batch as BigModel error dispatch (v0.4.6), and the multiple subsequent hotfix bundles (C through F) suggest the integration was not stable. Key risks: (1) retry loops that don't respect signal/abort on process shutdown, leaving zombie requests; (2) backoff jitter that clusters under concurrent callers; (3) retry-after header parsing that silently falls through to default behavior on malformed values, producing either no retries or retry storms.
findings.recommendation: Audit retry.mjs for: (a) AbortSignal propagation on every sleep/wait, (b) bounded maximum retry count enforced independently of backoff logic, (c) safe parsing of Retry-After headers with fallback to configured defaults, (d) test coverage for concurrent callers, signal abort mid-retry, and malformed server responses. The existing tests/retry.test.mjs (352 lines) should be checked for these specific scenarios.

findings.title: glm-client.mjs heavy modification (155 insertions) with error dispatch rewrite may swallow errors
findings.body: glm-client.mjs was significantly rewritten (155 insertions alongside the new bigmodel-errors.mjs dispatch system). The risk is that error classification in bigmodel-errors.mjs may not cover all HTTP status codes or response shapes, causing errors to be silently classified as retryable or ignorable when they should surface to the user. This is exactly the class of silent failure that is expensive to detect in production.
findings.recommendation: Verify bigmodel-errors.mjs has an exhaustive fallback classification that always surfaces truly unexpected errors. Check that every code path in glm-client.mjs either retries, throws, or logs — never silently drops an error. Add a test case for an unrecognized HTTP status code (e.g., 418, 503) to confirm it propagates correctly.

next_steps: Inspect the actual diff content of scripts/lib/process.mjs deletion and grep for any remaining imports of it
Read scripts/lib/retry.mjs and tests/retry.test.mjs in full; verify abort-signal propagation, bounded retry count, and malformed Retry-After handling
Read scripts/lib/bigmodel-errors.mjs and verify the error classification has a catch-all that never silently swallows unknown errors
Run the full test suite against the target branch and verify all 14 test files pass
Given the 6,600-line diff, consider a staged rollout rather than shipping the full cumulative change at once
